sections.paragraph:
διαταράσσω 1 aor. pass. διεταράχθην (X., Pla. et al.; Polyb. 11, 1, 9; Diod S 18, 7, 6 [pass.]; TestSim 4:9; Jos., Ant. 2, 120, Vi. 281 [pass.]; 3 Km 21:43 Sym.) confuse, perplex (greatly) ἐπὶ τῷ λόγῳ διεταράχθη she was greatly perplexed at the saying Lk 1:29.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

TestSim TestSim = Testament of Simeon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 237
